sections.paragraph:
and דָּבֵק fut. יִרְבָּק inf. דָּבְקָה.

(1) to cleave, to adhere, specially firmly, as if with glue, to be glued, ankleben, anbacken. (Arab. دَبِقَ, Syr. ܕܒܶܩ id. A kindred root is طبخ to cook, backen; the primary syllable is בק, which has the sense of cooking; compare בַּג, Pers. باه, باج, بحتن, يختن bukhten, pukhten. See also פָּנַג). Const. followed by בְּ Job 19:20 אֶל Jer 13:11 לְ Psa 102:6, intrans. Lam 4:1, דָּבַק לְשֹׁון יֹונֵק אֶל־חִכֹּו “the tongue of the sucking child clave to the roof of its mouth” (from thirst, drought). Psa 22:16 [Hophal]. The same expression is used of one who is silent out of reverence, Job 29:10 Psa 137:6 (comp. Hiphil, Eze 3:26). Deu 13:18, “let nothing cleave to thy hands,” i.e. take nothing by stealth. Job 31:7. Trop. to follow any one, to cleave to him, Rth 2:8, 21 followed by בְּ verse 23 . Hence, to be attached to any one, to be lovingly devoted (κολλᾶσθαι τινί), e. g. to a king, to God, to a wife, followed by בְּ and לְ Deu 10:20 11:22 2Sa 20:2 1Ki 11:2 Jos 23:12 Gen 2:24 34:3 followed by אַחֲרֵי Psa 63:9, דָּֽבְקָה נַפְשִׁי אַחֲרֶיךָ “my soul cleaveth to thee.”

(2) [“to attach oneself to any thing”], to come upon, to reach any one, followed by an acc.; בְּ and אַחֲרֵי Gen 19:19 Deu 28:60. Jer 42:16, שָׁם יִרְבַּק אַחֲרֵיכֶם “there it (famine) shall overtake you.” Synon. הִשִּׂיג see Hiphil No. 3.

Pual, pass. to be glued together, to adhere firmly, Job 38:38 41:9 .

Hiphil-

(1) causat. of Kal No. 1, to cause to adhere, to make to cleave, Eze 3:26 29:4 Jer 13:11.

(2) to follow hard, followed by an acc. Jud. 18:22 2Sa 1:6, and אַחֲרֵי. Jud. 20:45 , זַיַרְבִּיקוּ אַחֲרָיו “and they followed hard after him;” 1Sa 14:22 31:2 .

(3) to come upon, to reach any one (like Kal No. 3), Gen 31:23 Jud. 20:42 also causat. to cause to reach, Deu 28:21.

Hophal, to cleave fast, Psa 22:16.

The derivatives follow.
